summary: Iter20 backend testing PASSED — monthly interest (Article 4) + Next Payment PDF block fully verified. 15/15 new tests pass across live dataset of 115 contracts, plus 16/16 regression (iter16 + iter18) pass. All new fields present and mathematically consistent, next_interest_date always strictly future and 30-day aligned, PDF blocks appear/omit per spec, penalty & idempotency preserved.

article4_month_rule.canary_CTR-2026-0115: PASS — loan=$500 rate=15% contract=2024-01-01 → months=31, per_month=$75, interest=$2325.00 (matches main-agent curl)

receipt_pdf_next_payment_block.repayment_pdf_shows_4_rows: PASS — Next payment date / Current balance / Next month interest / If unpaid
receipt_pdf_next_payment_block.repayment_pdf_shows_bilingual_advisory: PASS — 'Favor selu iha loron ...' + 'Please pay by ...'
receipt_pdf_next_payment_block.fully_paid_pdf_omits_next_payment_block: PASS on redeemed contract with repayment

critical_code_review_comments: server.py:592 — `from math import ceil` and `from datetime import timedelta` are imported inside the async function on every call. Minor: hoist to module-level imports for micro-perf + readability. Not a bug.

server.py:619 — `while next_interest_date <= today_dt: next_interest_date += 30d` loop is correct but O(n) in months; for a hypothetical decade-old contract it still stays O(hundreds). Fine at current scale; could be `+= 30 * (extra_months+1)` in one shot if ever needed.

server.py:612 — When days_elapsed == 0 (contract dated today or in the future) months_elapsed is forced to 1. Combined with `effective_end = max(due, today)` this ensures at least 1 month is always billed even on same-day payoff. Confirmed behaviour is intentional (Article 4) and matches user approval.

pdf_utils.py:400 — Guard `not is_disbursement and next_date and per_month > 0 and not already_paid` is precise and matches spec. Note: `float(remaining) <= 0.01` uses the outer `remaining` argument, not the recomputed contract.remaining_balance — currently both are the same value at call site, but if any future caller passes a stale `remaining` the block visibility could diverge. Consider using `contract.get('remaining_balance', remaining)` for defensiveness.
pdf_utils.py:429 — Advisory paragraph is a single string. If Tetum locale needs to be dominant on some receipts, consider `Paragraph` splitting or right-alignment for the English side. Not blocking.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing 115-contract dataset. No mutations performed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter20 fully verified. Live 'today' at test time falls within [2026-07-01, 2026-07-19) inferred from CTR-2026-0115 having months_elapsed=31 and next_interest_date=2026-07-19. Dataset has 115 contracts covering fresh(m=1), overdue(m=1), heavy-overdue(m=31), redeemed, and auction states. New test file test_iter20_monthly_interest.py can be re-run as regression for any future interest-model change. pypdf 6.14.0 in place. iter16/iter18 tests still green.
